|url=https://www.wwe.com/shows/wwepayback/2016/roman-reigns-aj-styles |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160425033016/https://www.wwe.com/shows/wwepayback/2016/roman-reigns-aj-styles |archive-date=April 25, 2016 |access-date=May 28, 2023 |publisher=[[WWE]]}} At [[Payback (2016)|Payback]] on May 1, Reigns eventually defeated Styles by pinfall after the match was restarted twice (with Styles first winning by countout and then by disqualification), despite Gallows and Anderson's interference.{{Cite web |last=Melok |first=Bobby |date=May 1, 2016 |title=WWE World Heavyweight Champion Roman Reigns def. AJ Styles |url=https://www.wwe.com/shows/wwepayback/2016/roman-reigns-aj-styles-results |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160502134756/https://www.wwe.com/shows/wwepayback/2016/roman-reigns-aj-styles-results |archive-date=May 2, 2016 |access-date=May 28, 2023 |publisher=[[WWE]]}} He received a rematch against Reigns at [[Extreme Rules (2016)|Extreme Rules]] on May 22 in an [[Hardcore wrestling|Extreme Rules match]],{{Cite web |last=Benigno |first=Anthony |date=May 1, 2016 |title=WWE World Heavyweight Champion Roman Reigns vs. AJ Styles (Extreme Rules Match) |url=https://www.wwe.com/shows/extremerules/2016/roman-reigns-aj-styles |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160503235425/https://www.wwe.com/shows/extremerules/2016/roman-reigns-aj-styles |archive-date=May 3, 2016 |access-date=May 28, 2023 |publisher=[[WWE]]}} but was unsuccessful in winning the championship due to interference from both Gallows and Anderson and [[The Usos]] (Jey and Jimmy Uso).{{Cite web |last=Melok |first=Bobby |date=May 22, 2016 |title=WWE World Heavyweight Champion Roman Reigns def. AJ Styles (Extreme Rules Match) |url=https://www.wwe.com/shows/extremerules/2016/roman-reigns-aj-styles-extreme-rules-match-results |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160525211937/https://www.wwe.com/shows/extremerules/2016/roman-reigns-aj-styles-extreme-rules-match-results |archive-date=May 25, 2016 |access-date=May 28, 2023 |publisher=[[WWE]]}} On the May 30 episode of ''Raw'', Styles welcomed back a returning [[John Cena]], only to attack Cena with help from Gallows and Anderson, turning [[heel (professional wrestling)|heel]] in the process.{{cite web |last1=Caldwell |first1=James |date=May 30, 2016 |title=5/30 WWE Raw Results – Caldwell's Complete Live TV Report |url=http://www.pwtorch.com/site/2016/05/30/may30rawcaldwellcomplete/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160602003351/https://www.pwtorch.com/site/2016/05/30/may30rawcaldwellcomplete/ |archive-date=June 2, 2016 |access-date=May 31, 2016 |work=Pro Wrestling Torch |publisher=TDH Communications Inc.}} This set up a match between Styles and Cena at [[Money in the Bank (2016)|Money in the Bank]] on June 19,{{Cite web |last=Clapp |first=John |date=June 3, 2016 |title=John Cena vs. AJ Styles |url=https://www.wwe.com/shows/moneyinthebank/2016/john-cena-aj-styles |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160606004738/https://www.wwe.com/shows/moneyinthebank/2016/john-cena-aj-styles |archive-date=June 6, 2016 |access-date=May 28, 2023 |publisher=[[WWE]]}} which Styles won after interference from The Club.{{Cite web |last=Murphy |first=Ryan |date=June 19, 2016 |title=AJ Styles def. John Cena |url=https://www.wwe.com/shows/moneyinthebank/2016/john-cena-aj-styles-results |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160623054952/https://www.wwe.com/shows/moneyinthebank/2016/john-cena-aj-styles-results |archive-date=June 23, 2016 |access-date=May 28, 2023 |website= |publisher=[[WWE]]}} At [[Battleground (2016)|Battleground]] on July 24, The Club were defeated by Cena and [[Enzo and Cass|Enzo Amore and Big Cass]] in a six-man tag team match after Cena pinned Styles.{{Cite web |last=Powers |first=Kevin |date=July 24, 2016 |title=John Cena, Enzo Amore & Big Cass def. The Club |url=http://www.wwe.com/shows/wwebattleground/2016/john-cena-enzo-amore-big-cass-the-club-result |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160728021445/http://www.wwe.com/shows/wwebattleground/2016/john-cena-enzo-amore-big-cass-the-club-result |archive-date=July 28, 2016 |access-date=August 7, 2016 |publisher=[[WWE]]}} During the [[2016 WWE Draft]], Styles was drafted to the [[SmackDown (WWE brand)|SmackDown]] [[WWE brand extension|brand]] in the first round of picks, being the brand's second pick overall, while Gallows and Anderson were drafted to the [[Raw (WWE brand)|Raw]] brand, splitting up The Club.{{cite web |date=July 19, 2016 |title=2016 WWE Draft results: WWE officially ushers in New Era |url=http://www.wwe.com/2016-wwe-draft-results |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160720044640/http://www.wwe.com/2016-wwe-draft-results |archive-date=July 20, 2016 |access-date=July 19, 2016 |publisher=[[WWE]]}} He faced Cena once more at the [[SummerSlam (2016)|SummerSlam]] pay-per-view on August 21,{{Cite web |last=Clapp |first=John |date=August 2, 2016 |title=John Cena vs. AJ Styles: A fight for the franchise |url=https://www.wwe.com/shows/summerslam/2016/john-cena-vs-aj-styles |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160804191845/https://www.wwe.com/shows/summerslam/2016/john-cena-vs-aj-styles |archive-date=August 4, 2016 |access-date=May 28, 2023 |website= |publisher=[[WWE]]}} where he was victorious.{{Cite web |last=Adkins |first=Greg |date=August 21, 2016 |title=Could AJ Styles defeat John Cena without the help of his Club cohorts? |url=https://www.wwe.com/shows/summerslam/2016/john-cena-vs-aj-styles-result |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160823124742/https://www.wwe.com/shows/summerslam/2016/john-cena-vs-aj-styles-result |archive-date=August 23, 2016 |access-date=May 28, 2023 |website= |publisher=[[WWE]]}} After his victory over Cena, Styles claimed himself to be the "new face of WWE" and "the Face That Runs the Place".{{cite web |last=Barnett |first=Jake |date=August 23, 2016 |title=8/23 Barnett's WWE Smackdown Live TV Review: AJ Styles appears after defeating John Cena at SummerSlam, Nikki Bella returns |url=http://prowrestling.net/site/2016/08/23/823-barnetts-wwe-smackdown-live-tv-review-aj-styles-appears-after-defeating-john-cena-at-summerslam-nikki-bella-returns/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160825072337/http://prowrestling.net/site/2016/08/23/823-barnetts-wwe-smackdown-live-tv-review-aj-styles-appears-after-defeating-john-cena-at-summerslam-nikki-bella-returns/ |archive-date=August 25, 2016 |access-date=September 14, 2016 |work=Pro Wrestling Dot Net}} [[File:Shakedown 06 (31650240352) crop.jpg|thumb|left|upright|Styles as [[WWE Championship|WWE Champion]] in December 2016]] Due to his victory at SummerSlam, Styles immediately re-entered the renamed WWE World Championship picture and received another opportunity at the title at [[Backlash (2016)|Backlash]] against [[Jon Moxley|Dean Ambrose]].{{cite web |last1=Clapp |first1=John |date=August 23, 2016 |title=WWE World Champion Dean Ambrose vs. AJ Styles |url=http://www.wwe.com/shows/backlash/wwe-world-champion-dean-ambrose-vs-aj-styles |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160827233523/http://www.wwe.com/shows/backlash/wwe-world-champion-dean-ambrose-vs-aj-styles |archive-date=August 27, 2016 |access-date=August 23, 2016 |publisher=[[WWE]]}} At Backlash on September 11, Styles defeated Ambrose to become the WWE World Champion for the first time after a [[Professional wrestling attacks#Low blow|low blow]], also his first championship in WWE and eighth world title overall.{{cite web |last1=Pappolla |first1=Ryan |date=September 11, 2016 |title=AJ Styles def. Dean Ambrose to win the WWE World Championship |url=http://www.wwe.com/shows/backlash/2016/dean-ambrose-vs-aj-styles-results |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160915062142/http://www.wwe.com/shows/backlash/2016/dean-ambrose-vs-aj-styles-results |archive-date=September 15, 2016 |access-date=September 11, 2016 |publisher=[[WWE]]}} Styles made his first successful title defense against Ambrose in a rematch on the September 27 episode of ''SmackDown Live'' after interference from Cena.{{cite web |last=Parks |first=Greg |date=September 27, 2016 |title=9/27 WWE Smackdown LIVE – Parks's Complete, Real-Time Report |url=http://www.pwtorch.com/site/2016/09/27/927-wwe-smackdown-live-parkss-ongoing-real-time-report/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160929222653/http://www.pwtorch.com/site/2016/09/27/927-wwe-smackdown-live-parkss-ongoing-real-time-report/ |archive-date=September 29, 2016 |access-date=September 27, 2016 |work=Pro Wrestling Torch |publisher=TDH Communications Inc.}} At [[No Mercy (2016)|No Mercy]] on October 9, Styles retained the title against Cena and Ambrose in a [[Professional wrestling match types#Basic non-elimination matches|triple threat match]] after pinning Cena.{{cite web |last1=Melok |first1=Bobby |date=October 9, 2016 |title=WWE World Champion AJ Styles def. Dean Ambrose and John Cena |url=http://www.wwe.com/shows/nomercy/2016/aj-styles-dean-ambrose-john-cena-results |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20161013162926/http://www.wwe.com/shows/nomercy/2016/aj-styles-dean-ambrose-john-cena-results |archive-date=October 13, 2016 |access-date=October 9, 2016 |publisher=[[WWE]]}} At [[Survivor Series (2016)|Survivor Series]] on November 20, Styles made up Team SmackDown alongside Ambrose, [[Bray Wyatt]], [[Randy Orton]] and [[Shane McMahon]] in the 5-on-5 [[Survivor Series match]], defeating Team Raw.{{Cite web |last=Burdick |first=Michael |date=November 20, 2016 |title=Team SmackDown LIVE def. Team Raw in a 5-on-5 Traditional Survivor Series Men's Elimination Match |url=https://www.wwe.com/shows/survivorseries/2016/raw-vs-smackdown-live-traditional-survivor-series-mens-elimination-match |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20161123075138/https://www.wwe.com/shows/survivorseries/2016/raw-vs-smackdown-live-traditional-survivor-series-mens-elimination-match |archive-date=November 23, 2016 |access-date=May 28, 2023 |publisher=[[WWE]]}} At [[TLC: Tables, Ladders & Chairs (2016)|TLC: Tables, Ladders & Chairs]] on December 4, Styles retained the championship over Ambrose in a [[Tables, Ladders, and Chairs match]] after [[James Ellsworth (wrestler)|James Ellsworth]] interfered and pushed Ambrose off a ladder and through several tables.{{cite web |last1=Melok |first1=Bobby |date=December 4, 2016 |title=WWE World Champion AJ Styles def. Dean Ambrose (Tables, Ladders and Chairs Match) |url=http://www.wwe.com/shows/wwetlc/2016/aj-styles-vs-dean-ambrose-tlc-match-results |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20161220170821/http://www.wwe.com/shows/wwetlc/2016/aj-styles-vs-dean-ambrose-tlc-match-results |archive-date=December 20, 2016 |access-date=December 4, 2016 |publisher=[[WWE]]}} Styles then resumed his feud with Cena leading into the [[Royal Rumble (2017)|Royal Rumble]] on January 29, 2017,{{cite web |last1=Parks |first1=Greg |date=December 27, 2016 |title=12/27 WWE Smackdown LIVE – Parks's Complete, Real-Time Report, including three title matches, John Cena's return |url=http://www.pwtorch.com/site/2016/12/27/1227-wwe-smackdown-live-parkss-ongoing-real-time-report-including-three-title-matches-john-cenas-return/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20161228194940/http://www.pwtorch.com/site/2016/12/27/1227-wwe-smackdown-live-parkss-ongoing-real-time-report-including-three-title-matches-john-cenas-return/ |archive-date=December 28, 2016 |access-date=December 27, 2016 |website=Pro Wrestling Torch |publisher=TDH Communications Inc.}}{{Cite web |last=Clapp |first=John |date=December 27, 2016 |title=WWE Champion AJ Styles vs. John Cena |url=https://www.wwe.com/shows/royalrumble/2017/aj-styles-vs-john-cena |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170126170807/https://www.wwe.com/shows/royalrumble/2017/aj-styles-vs-john-cena |archive-date=January 26, 2017 |access-date=May 28, 2023 |publisher=[[WWE]]}} where Styles was defeated for the championship, ending his reign at 140 days.{{cite web |last1=Adkins |first1=Greg |date=January 29, 2017 |title=John Cena def. AJ Styles to win the WWE Championship |url=http://www.wwe.com/shows/royalrumble/2017/aj-styles-vs-john-cena-results |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170201182405/http://www.wwe.com/shows/royalrumble/2017/aj-styles-vs-john-cena-results |archive-date=February 1, 2017 |access-date=January 29, 2017 |website= |publisher=[[WWE]]}} On February 12 at [[Elimination Chamber (2017)|Elimination Chamber]], Styles competed in an [[Elimination Chamber|Elimination Chamber match]] against Cena, [[Baron Corbin]], Wyatt, Ambrose and [[The Miz]] for the WWE Championship,{{Cite web |last=Clapp |first=John |date=January 29, 2017 |title=WWE Championship Elimination Chamber Match |url=https://www.wwe.com/shows/eliminationchamber/2017/wwe-championship-elimination-chamber-match |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170308044820/https://www.wwe.com/shows/eliminationchamber/2017/wwe-championship-elimination-chamber-match |archive-date=March 8, 2017 |access-date=May 30, 2023 |publisher=[[WWE]]}} which Wyatt won.{{Cite web |last=Melok |first=Bobby |date=February 12, 2017 |title=Bray Wyatt def. John Cena, The Miz, Baron Corbin, AJ Styles and Dean Ambrose in a WWE Championship Elimination Chamber Match |url=https://www.wwe.com/shows/eliminationchamber/2017/wwe-championship-elimination-chamber-match-results |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170213165411/https://www.wwe.com/shows/eliminationchamber/2017/wwe-championship-elimination-chamber-match-results |archive-date=February 13, 2017 |access-date=May 30, 2023 |publisher=[[WWE]]}} On the following episode of ''SmackDown Live'', Styles failed to regain the title from Wyatt in a triple threat match also involving Cena.{{cite web |last=Keller |first=Wade |date=February 14, 2017 |title=KELLER'S WWE SMACKDOWN REPORT 2/14: Ongoing coverage of Bray defending WWE Title against Cena, Elimination Chamber follow-up |url=http://www.pwtorch.com/site/2017/02/14/kellers-wwe-smackdown-report-214-ongoing-coverage-bray-defending-wwe-title-cena-elimination-chamber-follow/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170428145142/http://www.pwtorch.com/site/2017/02/14/kellers-wwe-smackdown-report-214-ongoing-coverage-bray-defending-wwe-title-cena-elimination-chamber-follow/ |archive-date=April 28, 2017 |access-date=February 14, 2017 |work=Pro Wrestling Torch |publisher=TDH Communications Inc.}} In March, Styles began a feud with SmackDown Commissioner Shane McMahon, due |publisher=[[WWE]]}}{{cite web |last=Pappolla |first=Ryan |date=March 14, 2017 |title=Shane McMahon seemingly called AJ Styles out for WrestleMania |url=http://www.wwe.com/shows/smackdown/2017-03-14/article/shane-mcmahon-seemingly-challenged-aj-styles-to-a-match-at |access-date=March 15, 2017 |publisher=[[WWE]]}} At [[WrestleMania 33]] on April 2, Styles defeated McMahon.{{cite web |last=Burdick |first=Michael |date=April 2, 2017 |title=AJ Styles def. Shane McMahon |url=http://www.wwe.com/shows/wrestlemania/33/shane-mcmahon-vs-aj-styles-results |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170430012551/http://www.wwe.com/shows/wrestlemania/33/shane-mcmahon-vs-aj-styles-results |archive-date=April 30, 2017 |access-date=April 2, 2017 |publisher=[[WWE]]}} On the following episode of ''SmackDown Live'', Styles shook hands with McMahon, turning [[face (professional wrestling)|face]] in the process.{{cite web |last=Keller |first=Wade |date=April 4, 2017 |title=KELLER'S WWE SMACKDOWN REPORT 4/4: WrestleMania 33 fallout including celebration interview with Orton, rematches, Miz & Maryse, and some NXT call-ups! |url=http://www.pwtorch.com/site/2017/04/04/kellers-wwe-smackdown-report-44-wrestlemania-33-fallout-including-celebration-interview-orton-rematches-miz-maryse-call-cena-nikki/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170406201303/http://www.pwtorch.com/site/2017/04/04/kellers-wwe-smackdown-report-44-wrestlemania-33-fallout-including-celebration-interview-orton-rematches-miz-maryse-call-cena-nikki/ |archive-date=April 6, 2017 |access-date=April 5, 2017 |work=Pro Wrestling Torch |publisher=TDH Communications Inc.}} On the April 11 episode of ''SmackDown Live'', Styles defeated Corbin and [[Sami Zayn]] to become the number one contender for the [[WWE United States Championship|United States Championship]] at [[Backlash (2017)|Backlash]] on May 21,{{cite web |last1=Keller |first1=Wade |date=April 11, 2017 |title=KELLER'S WWE SMACKDOWN REPORT 4/11: Superstar Shake-up Night 2 details |url=http://www.pwtorch.com/site/2017/04/11/kellers-wwe-smackdown-report-411-superstar-shake-night-2-details/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170413150854/http://www.pwtorch.com/site/2017/04/11/kellers-wwe-smackdown-report-411-superstar-shake-night-2-details/ |archive-date=April 13, 2017 |access-date=April 11, 2017 |work=Pro Wrestling Torch |publisher=TDH Communications Inc.}}{{Cite web |last=Burdick |first=Michael |date=April 25, 2017 |title=United States Champion Kevin Owens vs. AJ Styles |url=https://www.wwe.com/shows/backlash/2017/aj-styles-kevin-owens-united-states-title-match |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170519163146/https://www.wwe.com/shows/backlash/2017/aj-styles-kevin-owens-united-states-title-match |archive-date=May 19, 2017 |access-date=May 30, 2023 |publisher=[[WWE]]}} but lost his match for the title against Kevin Owens by countout.{{Cite web |last=Benigno |first=Anthony |date=May 21, 2017 |title=United States Champion Kevin Owens def. AJ Styles via count-out |url=https://www.wwe.com/shows/backlash/2017/kevin-owens-aj-styles-results |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170527013714/https://www.wwe.com/shows/backlash/2017/kevin-owens-aj-styles-results |archive-date=May 27, 2017 |access-date=May 30, 2023 |publisher=[[WWE]]}} After Styles again became the number one contender to Owens' championship,{{cite web |last=McNeill |first=Pat |date=July 4, 2017 |title=McNEILL'S WWE SMACKDOWN REPORT 7/4: John Cena Returns, Rap Battle, Naomi-Lana III & Battle Royal |url=http://www.pwtorch.com/site/2017/07/04/mcneills-wwe-smackdown-report-74-john-cena-returns-rap-battle-naomi-lana-iii-battle-royal/ |archive-date=July 22, 2017 |access-date=July 4, 2017 |work=Pro Wrestling Torch |publisher=TDH Communications Inc.}} a title match was set for [[Battleground (2017)|Battleground]].{{cite web |last1=Taylor |first1=Scott |date=July 11, 2017 |title=United States Champion AJ Styles vs. Kevin Owens |url=http://www.wwe.com/shows/wwebattleground/2017/aj-styles-kevin-owens |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170727014522/http://www.wwe.com/shows/wwebattleground/2017/aj-styles-kevin-owens |archive-date=July 27, 2017 |access-date=July 11, 2017 |publisher=[[WWE]]}} However, on July 7, two weeks before the pay-per-view, Styles defeated Owens during a [[house show]] at [[Madison Square Garden]] to capture the United States Championship for the first time in his career.{{cite web |last1=Melok |first1=Bobby |date=July 7, 2017 |title=AJ Styles wins United States Championship from Kevin Owens at Madison Square Garden Live Event |url=http://www.wwe.com/shows/smackdown/article/aj-styles-wins-us-title-madison-square-garden |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170729054321/http://www.wwe.com/shows/smackdown/article/aj-styles-wins-us-title-madison-square-garden |archive-date=July 29, 2017 |access-date=July 7, 2017 |publisher=[[WWE]]}} At Battleground on July 23, Styles lost the title back to Owens,{{Cite web |last=Benigno |first=Anthony |date=July 23, 2017 |title=Kevin Owens def. AJ Styles to become the new United States Champion |url=https://www.wwe.com/shows/wwebattleground/2017/aj-styles-kevin-owens-results |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170727093511/https://www.wwe.com/shows/wwebattleground/2017/aj-styles-kevin-owens-results |archive-date=July 27, 2017 |access-date=May 30, 2023 |publisher=[[WWE]]}} but regained it two days later on ''SmackDown Live'' in a triple threat match also involving a returning Chris Jericho.{{cite web |last=Keller |first=Wade |date=July 25, 2017 |title=KELLER'S WWE SMACKDOWN REPORT 7/25: Surprise return, Owens gloats and Styles wants rematch, Nakamura vs. Corbin |url=http://www.pwtorch.com/site/2017/07/25/kellers-wwe-smackdown-report-725-surprise-return-owens-gloats-styles-wants-rematch-nakamura-vs-corbin/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170730025342/http://www.pwtorch.com/site/2017/07/25/kellers-wwe-smackdown-report-725-surprise-return-owens-gloats-styles-wants-rematch-nakamura-vs-corbin/ |archive-date=July 30, 2017 |access-date=July 25, 2017 |work=Pro Wrestling Torch |publisher=TDH Communications Inc.}} At [[SummerSlam (2017)|SummerSlam]] on August 20, Styles retained the title against Owens with Shane McMahon as the special guest referee.{{cite web |last1=Melok |first1=Bobby |date=August 20, 2017 |title=United States Champion AJ Styles def. Kevin Owens (Special Guest Referee Shane McMahon) |url=http://www.wwe.com/shows/summerslam/2017/aj-styles-kevin-owens-results |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170821092057/http://www.wwe.com/shows/summerslam/2017/aj-styles-kevin-owens-results |archive-date=August 21, 2017 |access-date=August 20, 2017 |publisher=[[WWE]]}} Styles once again defeated Owens two days later on ''SmackDown Live'' to end the feud.{{cite web |last=Keller |first=Wade |date=August 22, 2017 |title=KELLER'S WWE SMACKDOWN REPORT 8/22: Summerslam fallout including Owens-Styles-Shane controversy and a new roster member |url=http://www.pwtorch.com/site/2017/08/22/kellers-wwe-smackdown-report-822-summerslam-fallout-including-owens-styles-shane-controversy-new-roster-member/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170824064525/http://www.pwtorch.com/site/2017/08/22/kellers-wwe-smackdown-report-822-summerslam-fallout-including-owens-styles-shane-controversy-new-roster-member/ |archive-date=August 24, 2017 |access-date=August 22, 2017 |work=Pro Wrestling Torch |publisher=TDH Communications Inc.}} In September, he began a feud with [[Shawn Spears|Tye Dillinger]] and [[Baron Corbin]],{{cite web |last=Keller |first=Wade |date=August 29, 2017 |title=KELLER'S WWE SMACKDOWN REPORT 8/29: Jinder & Rusev vs. Orton & Nakamura headlines, plus A.J. Styles U.S. Title Open Challenge, Shelton & Gable |url=http://www.pwtorch.com/site/2017/08/29/kellers-wwe-smackdown-report-829-jinder-rusev-vs-orton-nakamura-headlines-plus-j-styles-u-s-title-open-challenge-shelton-gable/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170830102704/http://www.pwtorch.com/site/2017/08/29/kellers-wwe-smackdown-report-829-jinder-rusev-vs-orton-nakamura-headlines-plus-j-styles-u-s-title-open-challenge-shelton-gable/ |archive-date=August 30, 2017 |access-date=September 5, 2017 |work=Pro Wrestling Torch |publisher=TDH Communications Inc.}}{{cite web |last=Keller |first=Wade |date=September 12, 2017 |title=KELLER'S WWE SMACKDOWN REPORT 9/12: Vince McMahon appears to address Shane being suspended and Owens threatening to sue |url=http://www.pwtorch.com/site/2017/09/12/kellers-wwe-smackdown-report-912-vince-mcmahon-appears-address-shane-suspended-owens-threatening-sue/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170914220328/http://www.pwtorch.com/site/2017/09/12/kellers-wwe-smackdown-report-912-vince-mcmahon-appears-address-shane-suspended-owens-threatening-sue/ |archive-date=September 14, 2017 |access-date=September 12, 2017 |work=Pro Wrestling Torch |publisher=TDH Communications Inc.}} leading to a triple threat match at [[Hell in a Cell (2017)|Hell in a Cell]] on October 8,{{cite web |last1=Benigno |first1=Anthony |date=September 26, 2017 |title=United States Champion AJ Styles vs. Baron Corbin vs. Tye Dillinger (Triple Threat Match) |url=http://www.wwe.com/shows/hellinacell/2017/aj-styles-vs-baron-corbin |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171101001040/https://www.wwe.com/shows/hellinacell/2017/aj-styles-baron-corbin-tye-dillinger |archive-date=November 1, 2017 |access-date=September 26, 2017 |publisher=[[WWE]]}} where Styles lost the title to Corbin who pinned Dillinger, ending his reign at 75 days.{{cite web |last1=Benigno |first1=Anthony |date=October 8, 2017 |title=Baron Corbin def. AJ Styles and Tye Dillinger to become the new United States Champion |url=http://www.wwe.com/shows/hellinacell/2017/aj-styles-baron-corbin-tye-dillinger-results |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171012034112/http://www.wwe.com/shows/hellinacell/2017/aj-styles-baron-corbin-tye-dillinger-results |archive-date=October 12, 2017 |access-date=October 8, 2017 |publisher=[[WWE]]}} Two days later on ''SmackDown Live'', Styles failed to regain the title from Corbin.{{cite web |last=Keller |first=Wade |date=October 10, 2017 |title=KELLER'S WWE SMACKDOWN REPORT 10/10: Sami Zayn has some explaining to do, Owens-Shane fallout, Styles challenges Corbin |archive-date=October 15, 2017 |access-date=October 11, 2017 |work=Pro Wrestling Torch |publisher=TDH Communications Inc.}} ==== Year-long WWE Championship reign (2017–2018) ==== [[File:Styles WWE Champion WM34 crop.jpg|thumb|right|Styles as WWE Champion at [[WrestleMania 34]] in April 2018|265x265px]] On the October 17 episode of ''SmackDown Live'', Styles began a rivalry with then WWE Champion [[Jinder Mahal]], confronting and attacking him.{{cite web |last=Barnett |first=Jake |date=October 17, 2017 |title=10/17 Barnett's WWE Smackdown Live TV Review: Jinder Mahal's Survivor Series announcement, Bobby Roode vs. Dolph Ziggler, Sami Zayn opens the show |url=https://prowrestling.net/site/2017/10/17/1017-barnetts-wwe-smackdown-live-tv-review-jinder-mahals-survivor-series-announcement-bobby-roode-vs-dolph-ziggler-sami-zayn-opens-show/ |archive-date=October 18, 2017 |access-date=October 17, 2017 |work=Pro Wrestling Dot Net}} On October 22, Styles appeared at Raw's exclusive event [[TLC: Tables, Ladders & Chairs (2017)|TLC: Tables, Ladders & Chairs]], competing against [[Finn Bálor]] due to Bálor's original opponent Bray Wyatt not being cleared to compete,{{cite web |last1=Benigno |first1=Anthony |date=October 20, 2017 |title="The Demon" Finn Bálor vs. AJ Styles |url=https://www.wwe.com/shows/wwetlc/2017/the-demon-finn-balor-vs-aj-styles |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171023033915/https://www.wwe.com/shows/wwetlc/2017/the-demon-finn-balor-vs-aj-styles |archive-date=October 23, 2017 |access-date=October 16, 2017 |publisher=[[WWE]]}}{{cite web |last=Johnson |first=Mike |date=October 20, 2017 |title=WWE scrambling as TLC card is revamped due to medical issues; Kurt Angle to return to the ring this Sunday; Finn Bálor def. AJ Styles |url=http://www.wwe.com/shows/wwetlc/2017/the-demon-finn-balor-aj-styles-results |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171023184508/http://www.wwe.com/shows/wwetlc/2017/the-demon-finn-balor-aj-styles-results |archive-date=October 23, 2017 |access-date=October 22, 2017 |publisher=[[WWE]]}} On the November 7 episode of ''SmackDown Live'' in [[Manchester]], England, Styles defeated Mahal to win the WWE Championship for the second time in what marked the first time the title changed hands on ''SmackDown'' since September 2003, becoming recognized by WWE as the first titleholder crowned outside of [[North America]]{{cite web |last=Pappolla |first=Ryan |date=November 7, 2017 |title=AJ Styles def. Jinder Mahal to win the WWE Championship |url=http://www.wwe.com/shows/smackdown/2017-11-07/article/aj-styles-def-jinder-mahal |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171108165939/http://www.wwe.com/shows/smackdown/2017-11-07/article/aj-styles-def-jinder-mahal |archive-date=November 8, 2017 |access-date=November 8, 2017 |publisher=[[WWE]]}} ([[Antonio Inoki]] had won the title in Japan in 1979,{{cite web |title=WWWF/WWF/WWE Heavyweight Title History |url=http://www.thehistoryofwwe.com/world_title.htm |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180111120651/http://www.thehistoryofwwe.com/world_title.htm |archive-date=January 11, 2018 |access-date=November 8, 2017 |publisher=The History of WWE}} but this reign is unrecognized by WWE). With the win, Styles replaced Mahal in the champion vs. champion match at [[Survivor Series (2017)|Survivor Series]] against [[WWE Universal Championship|Universal Champion]] [[Brock Lesnar]] on November 19,{{cite web |last=Burdick |first=Michael |date=November 7, 2017 |title=Universal Champion Brock Lesnar vs. WWE Champion AJ Styles (Champion vs. Champion Match) |url=http://www.wwe.com/shows/survivorseries/2017/brock-lesnar-vs-aj-styles |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171108212718/http://www.wwe.com/shows/survivorseries/2017/brock-lesnar-vs-aj-styles |archive-date=November 8, 2017 |access-date=November 7, 2017 |publisher=[[WWE]]}} but lost.{{cite web |last=Benigno |first=Anthony |date=November 19, 2017 |title=Universal Champion Brock Lesnar def. WWE Champion AJ Styles |url=http://www.wwe.com/shows/survivorseries/2017/brock-lesnar-aj-styles-results |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171201051632/http://www.wwe.com/shows/survivorseries/2017/brock-lesnar-aj-styles-results |archive-date=December 1, 2017 |access-date=November 19, 2017 |publisher=[[WWE]]}} At [[Clash of Champions (2017)|Clash of Champions]] on December 17, Styles defeated Mahal via submission in his first title defense.{{cite web |last=Benigno |first=Anthony |date=December 17, 2017 |title=WWE Champion AJ Styles def. |publisher=[[WWE]]}} His next feud came against Kevin Owens and [[Sami Zayn]], whom he lost back-to-back matches to due to interference,{{cite web |last1=Barnett |first1=Jake |date=December 26, 2017 |access-date=December 26, 2017 |work=Pro Wrestling Dot Net}}{{cite web |last1=Barnett |first1=Jake |date=January 2, 2018 |title=01/02 Barnett's WWE Smackdown Live TV Review: WWE Champion AJ Styles vs. WWE Champion AJ Styles by disqualification |url=https://www.wwe.com/shows/summerslam/summerslam-2018/aj-styles-samoa-joe-results |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181004145902/https://www.wwe.com/shows/summerslam/summerslam-2018/aj-styles-samoa-joe-results |archive-date=October 4, 2018 |access-date=May 30, 2023 |publisher=[[WWE]]}} Styles' and Joe's rivalry continued over Styles' wife, Wendy, where Joe claimed he would defeat Styles and become the "new daddy",{{cite web |last=Barnett |first=Jake |date=August 21, 2018 |title=8/21 Barnett's WWE Smackdown Live TV Review: Bludgeon Brothers vs. New Day in a No DQ match for the Smackdown Tag Titles, Randy Orton vs. Jeff Hardy, Becky Lynch explains her actions at SummerSlam, AJ Styles interview |url=https://prowrestling.net/site/2018/08/21/8-21-barnetts-wwe-smackdown-live-tv-review-bludgeon-brothers-vs-new-day-in-a-no-dq-match-for-the-smackdown-tag-titles-randy-orton-vs-jeff-hardy-becky-lynch-explains-her-actions-at-summerslam-a/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180822050234/https://prowrestling.net/site/2018/08/21/8-21-barnetts-wwe-smackdown-live-tv-review-bludgeon-brothers-vs-new-day-in-a-no-dq-match-for-the-smackdown-tag-titles-randy-orton-vs-jeff-hardy-becky-lynch-explains-her-actions-at-summerslam-a/ |archive-date=August 22, 2018 |access-date=August 21, 2018 |work=Pro Wrestling Dot Net}} leading to a rematch at [[Hell in a Cell (2018)|Hell in a Cell]] on September 16,{{Cite web |last=Burdick |first=Michael |date=August 24, 2018 |title=WWE Champion AJ Styles vs. Samoa Joe |url=https://www.wwe.com/shows/hellinacell/2018/aj-styles-samoa-joe |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181004125620/https://www.wwe.com/shows/hellinacell/2018/aj-styles-samoa-joe |archive-date=October 4, 2018 |access-date=May 30, 2023 |publisher=[[WWE]]}} which Styles won in [[glossary of professional wrestling terms#screwjob|controversial fashion]] to retain the title; |publisher=[[WWE]]}} Styles retained the title against Joe at [[Super Show-Down (2018)|Super Show-Down]] on October 6 by submission{{Cite web |last=Pappolla |first=Ryan |date=October 6, 2018 |website= |publisher=[[WWE]]}} and [[Crown Jewel (2018)|Crown Jewel]] on November 2.{{Cite web |last=Benigno |first=Anthony |date=November 2, 2018 |title=AJ Styles holds off Samoa Joe once again at |access-date=September 1, 2025 |work=[[PWTorch]]}} ==== Retirement plans (2025–present) ==== On September 23, WWE announced that Styles would face John Cena at [[Crown Jewel (2025)|Crown Jewel]].{{cite web|author=WWE.com Staff|title=John Cena vs. AJ Styles|url=https://www.wwe.com/crown-jewel-2025/john-cena-vs-aj-styles|work=[[WWE]]|date=September 23, 2025|access-date=September 23, 2025}} In an interview with [[Tokyo Sports]] on September 26, Styles stated that he plans to retire soon, noting that his match at the ''WWE SuperShow Japan'' [[house show]] would likely be his final match in [[Japan]]. Although his contract is reportedly set to expire in February 2026, he mentioned the possibility of appearing at [[WrestleMania 42]]. Styles also revealed that he intends to work as a trainer in WWE following his in-ring retirement, though the decision has not yet been finalized.{{Cite web |date=September 26, 2025 |title=【ＷＷＥ】ＡＪスタイルズが２０２６年中の引退を表明「体が動けなくなる前に…」 ライバル棚橋弘至に越境メッセージ |url=https://www.tokyo-sports.co.jp/articles/-/360969 |access-date=October 10, 2025 |website=東スポWEB |language=ja}} On October 10, during the ''Crown Jewel: Perth'' ''Kickoff'' show, Styles confirmed his plans to retire in 2026, citing his concerns about no longer being able to perform at his expected level and his desire to spend more time with his family as reasons for his decision.{{Cite web |last=Toolan |first=Jamie |date=October 10, 2025 |title=AJ Styles WWE Retirement Update |url=https://wrestletalk.com/news/wwe-aj-styles-retirement-update/ |access-date=October 10, 2025 |website=WrestleTalk |language=en-US}} The following day, Styles lost his Crown Jewel match against Cena.{{cite web |last=Powell |first=Jason |date=October 11, 2025 |title=WWE Crown Jewel Perth results: Powell's review of Cody Rhodes vs. Seth Rollins for the men's Crown Jewel Championship, Stephanie Vaquer vs. Tiffany Stratton for the women's Crown Jewel Championship |url=https://prowrestling.net/site/2025/10/11/wwe-crown-jewel-perth-results-powells-review-of-cody-rhodes-vs-seth-rollins-for-the-mens-crown-jewel-championship-stephanie-vaquer-vs-tiffany-stratton-for-the-womens-crown-jewel-championship/ |access-date=October 11, 2025 |work=[[Pro Wrestling Dot Net]]}} On October 17 at the ''WWE SuperShow Japan'' house show, Styles wrestled his final match on Japanese soil, defeating [[Kofi Kingston]].{{Cite web |last=Reuter |first=Sean |date=October 22, 2025 |title=WWE gives us a great look at AJ Styles emotional Japanese farewell |url=https://www.cagesideseats.com/391881/aj-styles-emotional-goodbye-japan-wwe-video-nakamura-punk-tokyo-retire |access-date=October 23, 2025 |website=Cageside Seats}} Three nights later on ''Raw'', Styles and [[Dragon Lee (wrestler)|Dragon Lee]] won the [[World Tag Team Championship (WWE)|World Tag Team Championship]] by defeating [[Finn Bálor]] and [[JD McDonagh]] of [[The Judgment Day]].{{Cite news |last=Russell |first=Skylar |date=October 21, 2025 |title=AJ Styles & Dragon Lee Win WWE World Tag Titles On WWE Raw |url=https://www.fightful.com/wrestling/aj-styles-and-dragon-lee-win-wwe-world-tag-team-championships-on-wwe-raw?xv=20251021004939 |work=Fightful News |access-date=October 21, 2025}} == Legacy == In a 2016 article, ''[[Pro Wrestling Torch]]'' journalist Michael Moore noted that Styles "has long been regarded as one of the best wrestlers in the world".{{cite web |last=Moore |first=Michael |date=September 1, 2016 |title=Where to Find Top Cards of 2016's Veteran Breakout Stars |url=http://www.pwtorch.com/site/2016/09/01/collectibles-column-find-top-cards-2016s-veteran-breakout-stars/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20161104113247/http://www.pwtorch.com/site/2016/09/01/collectibles-column-find-top-cards-2016s-veteran-breakout-stars/ |archive-date=November 4, 2016 |access-date=September 11, 2016 |website=Pro Wrestling Torch |publisher=TDH Communications Inc.}} In November 2012, ''[[IGN UK]]'' columnist Matt Fowler ranked Styles at number 38 in his top 50 professional wrestlers of all-time list.{{cite web |author=Fowler |first=Matt |date=November 2, 2012 |title=Top 50 Wrestlers of All Time |url=http://www.ign.com/articles/2012/11/02/top-50-wrestlers-of-all-time?page=2 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181116065215/https://www.ign.com/articles/top-50-wrestlers-of-all-time?page=2 |archive-date=November 16, 2018 |access-date=February 29, 2016 |website= |publisher=[[IGN]]}} Luke Winkie of ''[[Sports Illustrated]]'' listed him as the 46th greatest wrestler of all time in 2016, noting that: "Honestly, in a couple years it wouldn't surprise me if A.J. belongs in the top 20."{{cite magazine |last=Winkie |first=Luke |date=July 26, 2016 |title=Ranking the top 101 wrestlers of all time |url=https://www.si.com/extra-mustard/2016/07/26/wwe-wcw-ecw-100-best-wrestlers-all-time |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160731185657/http://www.si.com/extra-mustard/2016/07/26/wwe-wcw-ecw-100-best-wrestlers-all-time |archive-date=July 31, 2016 |access-date=August 1, 2016 |magazine=[[Sports Illustrated]]}} Ayub Nouinou, a columnist for ''[[The Independent]]'', described him as the "crown jewel" of TNA for more than a decade, before "applying his craft" for NJPW and ROH, among others.{{cite web |author=Nouinou, Ayub |date=January 22, 2016 |title=AJ Styles can be the superstar that spearheads WWE into a new era |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/wwe-mma-wrestling/wwe-comment-aj-styles-can-be-the-superstar-that-spearheads-wwe-into-a-new-era-a6828211.html |url-access=limited |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160126124848/http://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/wwe-mma-wrestling/wwe-comment-aj-styles-can-be-the-superstar-that-spearheads-wwe-into-a-new-era-a6828211.html |archive-date=January 26, 2016 |access-date=February 29, 2016 |work=[[The Independent]]}} Nouinou went on to state that Styles had "established himself as one of the best talents on the planet over the last two years (2014 and 2015)" and noted that pertaining to Styles' 2016 WWE signing "the addition of Styles would have to be considered one of the greatest signings since the turn of the millennium". Finally, Nouinou made the prediction that "Styles can be the superstar that spearheads WWE into a new era". Josh Barnett, a columnist for ''[[USA Today]]'', stated in March 2017 that Styles "could be considered the [[Most valuable player|MVP]] of WWE over the last year". Barnett also added that in 2016 Styles had "some of the WWE's best matches, including multiple matches against [[John Cena]] and [[Roman Reigns]]", while also noting that he "is considered one of the rare performers who can have a great match with anyone".{{cite web |last=Barnett |first=Josh |date=March 15, 2017 |title=Why A.J. Styles vs. Shane McMahon at WrestleMania might be better than you think |url=http://ftw.usatoday.com/2017/03/aj-styles-shane-mcmahon |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230325052348/http://ftw.usatoday.com/2017/03/aj-styles-shane-mcmahon |archive-date=March 25, 2023 |access-date=November 16, 2017 |website=[[USA Today]]}} By late 2021, Philip Lindsey of [[Bleacher Report]] wrote that Styles was considered "one of the best in-ring competitors in the world. Many top stars have had some of their most memorable matches opposite this high-flying technician."{{cite web |last1=Lindsey |first1=Philip |date=November 7, 2021 |title=Ranking the Top 15 WWE and AEW Stars Aged 40 and Above |url=https://bleacherreport.com/articles/2950216-ranking-the-top-15-wwe-and-aew-stars-aged-40-and-above |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230403223429/https://bleacherreport.com/articles/2950216-ranking-the-top-15-wwe-and-aew-stars-aged-40-and-above |archive-date=April 3, 2023 |access-date=February 23, 2022 |publisher=[[Bleacher Report]] |language=en}} Many of Styles' peers have praised him as being one of the greatest in-ring performers. [[Kurt Angle]] named him as the best wrestler that he ever wrestled, calling him a "rare breed".{{cite web |last=Atkin |first=Nicolas |date=February 27, 2016 |title=Ex-WWE star Kurt Angle: Family more important than wrestling now |url=http://www.espn.co.uk/olympics/story/_/id/14852102/ex-wwe-star-kurt-angle-tna-impact-retirement-family-more-important-wrestling |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230320013015/http://www.espn.co.uk/olympics/story/_/id/14852102/ex-wwe-star-kurt-angle-tna-impact-retirement-family-more-important-wrestling |archive-date=March 20, 2023 |access-date=February 28, 2016 |work=[[ESPN]]}} [[Stone Cold Steve Austin]] dubbed Styles "the best wrestler on two feet",{{cite episode|title=Chapter 5: Revelation|series=Undertaker: The Last Ride|network=[[WWE Network]]|date=June 21, 2020|minutes=16}} while [[The Undertaker]], whom Styles faced in his retirement match, described him as "probably the best in the business... [the] closest to [[Shawn Michaels]] that there is."{{cite episode|title=One More Round: The Undertaker|series=Steve Austin's Broken Skull Sessions|network=[[WWE Network]]|date=November 22, 2020|minutes=102}} The comparison to Shawn Michaels was also made by renowned [[professional wrestling promoter]] and [[Manager (professional wrestling)|manager]] [[Paul Heyman]], who stated that: "He's everything that Shawn Michaels, and [[Bret Hart]], and [[Ric Flair]] were to their generation and he's updated it. He's evolved their styles to truly be the single most phenomenal in-ring performer I have ever had the honour and the pleasure of watching up close".{{cite web |last=Jefferson |first=Lake |date=November 21, 2017 |title=AJ Styles is 'on a par with Ric Flair, Bret Hart and Shawn Michaels', says Paul Heyman |url=http://www.skysports.com/wwe/news/14203/11136810/aj-styles-is-on-a-par-with-ric-flair-bret-hart-and-shawn-michaels-says-paul-heyman |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190704101942/http://www.skysports.com/wwe/news/14203/11136810/aj-styles-is-on-a-par-with-ric-flair-bret-hart-and-shawn-michaels-says-paul-heyman |archive-date=July 4, 2019 |access-date=February 23, 2018 |website=[[Sky Sports]]}} == Other media == In 2003, ROH released ''Evolution of a Phenom: The Best of AJ Styles'', a DVD which covers Styles from his debut to mid-2003. In 2004, ROH released ''The Phenomenon Continues: The Best of AJ Styles Vol. 2'', a follow-up DVD to the original released in 2003. Also in 2004, TNA released ''Phenomenal: The Best of A.J. Styles'', a DVD covering Styles' best matches. In 2006, ROH released a [[Glossary of professional wrestling terms#Shoot|shoot interview]] featuring Styles, alongside [[Christopher Daniels]] for the promotions "Straight Shootin{{'"}} series. In 2007, TNA released ''Phenomenal: The Best of A.J. Styles Volume 2'', a follow-up DVD to the original released in 2004. Also in 2007, Styles was mentioned in the [[Insane Clown Posse]] song "Scatterbrain" off their EP ''[[Eye of the Storm (EP)|Eye of the Storm]]''. In 2008, Styles helped develop the first TNA wrestling video game known as ''[[TNA Impact! (video game)|TNA Impact!]]'', which was released the same year on September 9.{{cite web |date=October 14, 2007 |title=TNA Wrestlers do Motion Capture for TNA Impact Video Game |url=http://www.gamespot.com/video/933905/6180964/tna-impact-official-movie-2 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071023214852/http://www.gamespot.com/video/933905/6180964/tna-impact-official-movie-2 |archive-date=October 23, 2007 |access-date=April 2, 2008 |publisher=[[GameSpot]]}} In 2009, Styles was featured on [[MTV]]'s ''[[Made (TV series)|Made]]'' trying to help a young high schooler become a professional wrestler alongside [[Taylor Wilde]] and [[The Beautiful People (professional wrestling)|The Beautiful People]] ([[Angelina Love]] and [[Velvet Sky]]). In 2011, Styles was featured in the music video for country singer [[Sarah Darling]]'s song "Something to Do with Your Hands".{{cite web|url=http://www.wrestleview.com/viewnews.php?id=1297394973|title=Sarah Darling Tag Teams with AJ Styles in Video|last=Gerweck|first=Steve|date=February 10, 2011|work=WrestleView|access-date=February 15, 2011|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110220180550/http://www.wrestleview.com/viewnews.php?id=1297394973|archive-date=February 20, 2011}} In 2012, Styles and [[Montell Jordan]] presented the award for Best Rap/Hip Hop Record of the Year to [[Lecrae]] at the 43rd annual [[GMA Dove Award]]s.{{cite web |last=Caldwell |first=James |date=April 24, 2012 |title=Recap of Styles on Dove Awards show |url=http://pwtorch.com/artman2/publish/quicknews/article_60727.shtml |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120629000241/http://pwtorch.com/artman2/publish/quicknews/article_60727.shtml |archive-date=June 29, 2012 |access-date=July 21, 2012 |work=Pro Wrestling Torch |publisher=TDH Communications Inc.}} In 2013, ROH released ''AJ Styles - Styles Clash'', a 2-disc DVD featuring Styles' best ROH matches from 2002 to 2005. In 2016, TNA released ''The Essential AJ Styles Collection'', a 4-disc DVD set showcasing Styles' top 30 greatest TNA matches.{{cite web|url=http://shoptna.com/the-essential-aj-styles-collection.aspx|title=The Essential AJ Styles Collection|access-date=March 4, 2016|work=ShopTNA|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160305115923/http://shoptna.com/the-essential-aj-styles-collection.aspx|archive-date=March 5, 2016|url-status=dead}} In 2018, it was announced that Styles would be on the cover for ''[[WWE 2K19]]''.{{cite web|url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Uxc7hw7XU4Y |archive-url=https://ghostarchive.org/varchive/youtube/20211221/Uxc7hw7XU4Y |archive-date=December 21, 2021 |url-status=live|title=WWE 2K19 Press Conference @ 8:00 am PT|author=WWE 2K|publisher=[[YouTube]]|date=June 18, 2018|access-date=June 18, 2018|author-link=WWE 2K}}{{cbignore}} He selected the song "[[Survival (Eminem song)|Survival]]" by [[Eminem]] to appear in the soundtrack.{{cite web |date=September 18, 2018 |title=WWE 2K - News - WWE Superstars Select The WWE 2K19 Soundtrack; Now Streaming on Apple Music |url=https://wwe.2k.com/news/wwe-2k19-soundtrack/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191116051348/https://wwe.2k.com/news/wwe-2k19-soundtrack/ |archive-date=November 16, 2019 |access-date=May 30, 2023 |website= |publisher=[[WWE 2K]]}} === Filmography === ==== Television ==== {|class="wikitable sortable" |- !Year !Series !Role !Notes |- |2009 || ''[[Made (TV series)|Made]]'' || Himself{{cite web |last=Fowler |first=Matt |date=September 3, 2009 |title=AJ Styles Gets Made |url=http://www.ign.com/articles/2009/09/04/aj-styles-gets-made |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190409020529/https://www.ign.com/articles/2009/09/04/aj-styles-gets-made |archive-date=April 9, 2019 |access-date=May 30, 2023 |publisher=[[IGN]]}}|| Coach

|''[[Table for 3]]'' |Episode: "Common Bonds" |} == Personal life == Jones has been married to school teacher Wendy since 2000.{{cite news |author=Baines, Tim |date=February 13, 2005 |title=Styles still showing he's X-cellent champ |newspaper=[[Ottawa Sun]] |url=http://slam.canoe.ca/Slam/Wrestling/2005/02/13/929938-sun.html |archive-url=https://archive.today/20130115101800/http://slam.canoe.ca/Slam/Wrestling/2005/02/13/929938-sun.html |url-status=usurped |archive-date=January 15, 2013 |access-date=August 19, 2008 }} They have three sons: Ajay Covell Jones (born May 3, 2005), Avery Jones (born February 14, 2007), and Albey Jones (born September 15, 2009), and a daughter, Anney Jones (born October 8, 2014).* {{cite web |last=Nation |first=Ryan |title=Styles admits to Elevation X worries |url=http://slam.canoe.ca/Slam/Wrestling/2007/03/07/3709518.html |access-date=August 18, 2008 |work=Slam Wrestling Sports! |publisher=[[Canadian Online Explorer]]}} * {{cite web |last=Borash |first=Jeremy |author-link=Jeremy Borash |date=September 17, 2009 |title=Jeremy Borash (JeremyBorash) on Twitter |url=https://twitter.com/JeremyBorash/status/4058225833 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160701230918/https://twitter.com/JeremyBorash/status/4058225833 |archive-date=July 1, 2016 |access-date=September 18, 2009 |website=[[Twitter]]}}8 * {{cite web |last=Styles |first=A.J. |author-link=AJ Styles |date=October 8, 2015 |title=AJStyles.Org on Twitter: 1 year ago I was blessed with something I thought I'd never have, a daughter, my Princess Anney. |url=https://twitter.com/AJStylesOrg/status/652125904915795969 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160305125840/https://twitter.com/AJStylesOrg/status/652125904915795969 |archive-date=March 5, 2016 |access-date=November 16, 2015 |website=[[Twitter]]}} Their first son's middle name was taken from the last name of Jones' best friend and fellow wrestler [[Christopher Daniels]], whose real name is Daniel Covell. In 2010, Jones acquired a large tattoo down the right side of his torso which reads "AJ 05-03-05 02-14-07 09-15-09", representing his initials and birth dates of his first three children.{{cite web |last=Borash |first=Jeremy |author-link=Jeremy Borash |date=August 1, 2010 |title=First pic! AJ Styles got ink! Initials of his 3 sons and their birthdays |url=http://twitpic.com/2aocy8 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304043400/http://twitpic.com/2aocy8 |archive-date=March 4, 2016 |access-date=August 2, 2010 |website=[[Twitter]]}} In 2016, he added the birth date of his fourth child, reading "10-08-14".{{Cite web |last=Bucher |first=Chris |date=September 6, 2016 |title=WWE Superstar AJ Styles' Tattoos: What Do the Dates Mean? |url=https://heavy.com/sports/2016/09/wwe-superstar-aj-styles-tattoo-tattoos-on-torso-date-dates-what-do-mean-signify-initials-wife-kids/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160913122821/https://heavy.com/sports/2016/09/wwe-superstar-aj-styles-tattoo-tattoos-on-torso-date-dates-what-do-mean-signify-initials-wife-kids/ |archive-date=September 13, 2016 |access-date=February 21, 2023 |website=[[Heavy (website)|Heavy.com]]}} The family lives in [[Gainesville, Georgia]].{{cite web |author=Clevett |first=Jason |date=April 20, 2005 |title=AJ Styles ready for new challenges |url=http://slam.canoe.com/Slam/Wrestling/2005/04/20/1005803.html |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160310030821/http://slam.canoe.com/Slam/Wrestling/2005/04/20/1005803.html |archive-date=March 10, 2016 |access-date=August 19, 2008 |work=Slam! Wrestling |publisher=[[Canadian Online Explorer]]}} Jones is a devout [[Christians|Christian]], stating that in his life, "It's God first and family second."{{cite web |author=Clevett, Jason |date=May 30, 2003 |title=AJ Styles: Southern Gentleman and a star |url=http://slam.canoe.ca/Slam/Wrestling/2003/05/30/99561.html |archive-url=https://archive.today/20130115124826/http://slam.canoe.ca/Slam/Wrestling/2003/05/30/99561.html |url-status=usurped |archive-date=January 15, 2013 |access-date=August 19, 2008 |work=Slam! Wrestling |publisher=[[Canadian Online Explorer]]}}{{cite web|url=http://www.nzpwi.co.nz/home/index.php?option=com_content&task=view&id=1168&Itemid=88|access-date=August 18, 2008|last=Cardno|first=James|title=AJ Styles Interview|publisher=New Zealand Pro Wrestling Journalists|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20081016140530/http://www.nzpwi.co.nz/home/index.php?option=com_content&task=view&id=1168&Itemid=88|archive-date=October 16, 2008}} He is a fan of [[Christian hip hop]] music, and his favorite groups to listen to are the West Coast-based K2S, Lil' Raskull, FTF, and L.G. Wise. While Jones was working for TNA, he met [[GRITS]] members Stacey "Coffee" Jones and Teron "Bonafide" Carter at [[Universal Studios Florida]], TNA's former primary television taping location. Jones would become good friends with the duo, using their remix of his entrance music "I Am", later renamed "Get Ready to Fly").{{cite web |author= |date=April 6, 2018 |title=AJ Styles, Wrestlemania Superstar, Is a Huge Christian Hip-Hop Head |url=https://relevantmagazine.com/culture/sports/aj-styles-wrestlemania-superstar-huge-christian-hip-hop-head/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210116182104/https://relevantmagazine.com/culture/sports/aj-styles-wrestlemania-superstar-huge-christian-hip-hop-head/ |archive-date=January 16, 2021 |access-date=February 21, 2023 |work=[[Relevant (magazine)|Relevant]]}} Jones has been accused throughout his career of being [[Homophobia|homophobic]] and being prejudiced against [[LGBTQ|LGBTQ+ people]] due to his use of the anti-[[gay]] slur "[[faggot]]" on numerous occasions.{{Cite web |date=November 30, 2018 |title=WWE Star A.J. Styles Under Fire For Repeatedly Using Gay Slurs • Instinct Magazine |url=https://instinctmagazine.com/wwe-star-a-j-styles-under-fire-for-repeatedly-using-gay-slurs/ |access-date=June 17, 2024 |website=Instinct Magazine |language=en-US}}{{Cite web |date=June 11, 2020 |title=AJ Styles attacks CM Punk instead of supporting Black Lives Matter |url=https://dailyddt.com/2020/06/11/aj-styles-attacks-cm-punk-instead-supporting-black-lives-matter/ |access-date=June 17, 2024 |website=Daily DDT |language=en-US}}{{Cite web |title=A.J. Styles's Weird Right-Wing Web TV Appearance Went About As Well As Could Be Expected |url=https://deadspin.com/a-j-styless-weird-right-wing-web-tv-appearance-went-ab-1831962462/ |access-date=June 17, 2024 |website=Deadspin |language=en}} [[National Wrestling Alliance|NWA]] censored part of a [[Total Nonstop Action Wrestling|NWA TNA]] segment in 2003, due to his use of inappropriate language, particularly his directing of the slur at [[Disco Inferno (wrestler)|Disco Inferno]].{{Cite web |date=April 5, 2013 |title=NWA-TNA Weekly PPV #37 - Scott's Blog of Doom! |url=https://www.blogofdoom.com/2013/04/05/nwa-tna-weekly-ppv-37/ |access-date=June 20, 2025 |website=www.blogofdoom.com |language=en-US |quote=He then challenges Gilbertti to go to the ring within the next thirty seconds before he saws off his body parts. He then calls him a “Faggot” as the rest of SEX acts like high school kids reacting to a fight.}} Management gave no consequences to Jones, but he did apologize stating that the slur "is a word you just say when you're when [[goofing off]]."{{Cite web |date=May 30, 2003 |title=AJ Styles: Southern Gentleman and a star - Slam Wrestling |url=https://slamwrestling.net/index.php/2003/05/30/aj-styles-southern-gentleman-and-a-star/ |access-date=June 17, 2024 |language=en-US}} At a 2005 [[Ring of Honor]] show, Styles used the slur against an audience member during a match against [[Cima (wrestler)|Cima]].{{Cite web |last=Kreikenbohm |first=Philip |title=ROH Dragon Gate Invasion « Events Database « CAGEMATCH - The Internet Wrestling Database |url=https://www.cagematch.net/?id=1&nr=3383 |website=Cagematch.net}} During a backstage segment at a [[Pro Wrestling Guerrilla]] show, Styles interrupted a promo for [[Kevin Owens|Kevin Steen]] and used the slur against him.{{Cite AV media |url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=uyU9BZ8Hef4 |title=PWG: AJ Styles and Kevin Steen promo |date=June 7, 2007 |last=E C |access-date=June 20, 2025 |via=YouTube}} == Championships and accomplishments == {{multiple image |direction = vertical |image1 = AJ Double Champion.png |caption1 = Styles has won numerous titles wrestling in [[Professional wrestling promotion|international promotions]], such as [[Revolution Pro Wrestling]]'s [[RPW British Heavyweight Championship]] (right hand) and being a two-time [[IWGP Heavyweight Championship|IWGP Heavyweight Champion]] while with [[New Japan Pro-Wrestling]] (left hand). |image2 = AJ Styles World Champion January 2010.jpg |caption2 = In TNA, he won the [[Impact World Championship|TNA World Heavyweight Championship]] twice and the [[NWA World Heavyweight Championship]] three times. |image3 = A.J. Styles Television Champion cropped.jpg |caption3 = Winning the [[TNA Television Championship|TNA Legends/Television Championship]] (of which he is a two-time title holder) made him the first [[Grand Slam (professional wrestling)#TNA Grand Slam Champions|TNA Grand Slam Champion]]. |image4 = AJ_Styles,_December_2016_(cropped).jpg |caption4 = Styles is a two-time [[WWE Champion]]. }} *'''[[Lucha {{Short description|American trade magazine}} {{Use American English|date=November 2022}} {{Use mdy dates|date=November 2022}} {{Infobox magazine | title = Amusement Today | logo = Amusement Today logo.svg | logo_size = 250px | image_file = | image_size = | image_alt = | image_caption = | editor = Gary Slade | editor_title = Editor-in-Chief | previous_editor = | staff_writer = | photographer = | category = [[Amusement park]]s | circulation = 3,000 | publisher = Gary Slade | founder = Gary Slade | founded = 1997 | firstdate = {{Start date|1997|4}} | company = | country = United States | based = [[Arlington, Texas]] | language = English | website = [http://www.amusementtoday.com www.amusementtoday.com] | issn = | oclc = }} '''''Amusement Today''''' is a monthly periodical that features articles, news, pictures and reviews about all things relating to the [[amusement park]] industry, including parks, [[List of amusement rides|rides]], and ride manufacturers. The trade newspaper, which is based in [[Arlington, Texas]], United States, was founded in January 1997 by Gary Slade, Virgil E. Moore III and Rick Tidrow.{{cite journal | year = 1997 | title = Sale of Amusement Today Inc. completed | journal = Amusement Today | volume = 4 | issue = 11 | page = 23}} In 1997, ''Amusement Today'' won the Impact Award in the services category for "Best New Product" from the [[International Association of Amusement Parks and Attractions]] (IAAPA). A year later, in 1998, the magazine founded the '''Golden Ticket Awards''', for which it has become best known for throughout the amusement park industry. On January 2, 2001, Slade bought out his two partners, giving him sole ownership of the paper. The paper has two full-time and two part-time staff members at its Arlington office, along with two full-time writers and several freelance writers in various parts of the world.{{cite web|url=http://amusementtoday.com/contact-us-2/ |title=Amusement Today: Contact us|date=February 20, 2012}} == Golden Ticket Awards == {{split|Golden Ticket Awards|date=March 2023}} Every year, ''Amusement Today'' gives out awards to the best of the best in the amusement park industry in a ceremony known as the Golden Ticket Awards. The awards are handed out based on surveys given to experienced and well-traveled amusement park enthusiasts from around the world. The awards, which were first handed out in 1998, have been featured on the [[Discovery Channel]] and the [[Travel Channel]]. === 2025 winners === {{Hidden begin|title=2025 Golden Ticket Awards{{cite journal|title=Amusement Today – Golden Ticket Awards 2025|journal=Amusement Today|date=September 2025|volume=28|issue=6.2|pages=92|url=https://amusementtoday.com/issues/2025/2025gta/|accessdate=September 7, 2025|format=PDF}}|titlestyle=text-align: center;|border=#FFFF00 1px solid}}
